rationale: Both candidates independently surface the same latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py still calling the removed get_cache_scale), which is impressive and matches the ground-truth reviewer's oblique '5 call sites/#4808' reference. But Y also recovers ground-truth-specific nuances X misses entirely: the qwen2_old getattr-guard 'clean-up not regression' point, and a sharper version of lishunyang12's 'test doesn't really validate the mapping' concern (Y shows the delegated-loader test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader and would stay green even if the mapping broke). X pads its review with speculative 'dead code' claims (guards 'never' return None) that are strong unverified universals, and its REQUEST CHANGES verdict on a merged, twice-approved PR is miscalibrated relative to how reviewers actually scoped this work (deferred via #4808/#4891, not a blocker on #4810). Both are highly actionable with concrete file:line fixes, but Y's higher recall and better-grounded, better-calibrated findings make it the stronger review.
